executive_briefing.summary: Liberation Day tariff shock activates Cascade Protocol; global stress regime escalates to CRITICAL; EM Equities collapse to BEARISH; Bonds, Metals flip BEARISH — 6 of 8 asset classes now BEARISH
executive_briefing.one_number_to_watch: CME futures: 60% probability no Fed cuts through end-2026. If that number falls below 40% (cut expectations return), it means the market has priced in a hard landing and expects emergency easing — the Fast Cascade scenario is underway.
executive_briefing.one_indicator_lying: Earnings revisions (+12.5% Q1 est.) remain GREEN with Blind Spot Rule 1 active. These figures pre-date tariff impact on corporate guidance. Q1 earnings season begins this week — watch for downside guidance from export-exposed sectors as the first real read-through of Liberation Day.

scenario_analysis.name: Slow Burn
scenario_analysis.description: Tariff shock absorbed gradually; Fed on hold; credit stress contained to private markets; EM equities and bonds under sustained pressure; stagflation regime persists through Q2 2026

scenario_analysis.label: Fast Cascade
scenario_analysis.name: Credit Contagion
scenario_analysis.description: EM currency crisis triggered by tariff demand destruction; private credit gate events escalate; VIX spikes above 40; Fed forced to emergency cut; equities -20%+

scenario_analysis.label: De-escalation
scenario_analysis.name: Policy Pivot
scenario_analysis.description: WTO dispute success, court injunction on IEEPA tariff authority, or US-China framework deal; relief rally in EM and bonds; stagflation premium compresses

key_judgments.judgment: Federal Reserve and European Central Bank are both frozen under dual mandate paralysis driven by energy price shock and tariff pass-through, not in a cyclical pause. Market pricing of 1-2 cuts in H2 2026 is inconsistent with the dual mandate constraint that neither central bank can cut while energy-driven inflation remains elevated and tariff pass-through to goods prices is still active.

USTR launch of 76 new Section 301 investigations is a systematic reconstitution of the tariff architecture on a more legally durable statutory basis following the Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against IEEPA-based tariffs. Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward.

ECB Vice-President flagging of deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves (nearly halved from 2022 peak to 2.6 trillion euros) is a liquidity stress signal at the euro area banking system level. Combined with the ECB stagflationary bind (inflation up, growth down), this raises the probability of a euro area financial stability event in H2 2026 if energy prices remain elevated or if a sovereign debt stress episode materializes. This is underweighted relative to its structural significance.

US-China trade decoupling is structural, not cyclical. Real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone, ending 40% below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels. China has actively diversified agricultural sourcing away from the US. US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since the 2008-09 global financial crisis. This is a regime-level shift, not a trade adjustment.

flags.title: Hyperscaler capex trajectory reinforces AI Infrastructure Debt indicator
flags.body: MAINTAINED: OpenAI $122B total funding at $852B valuation. Tariff shock applies directly to semiconductor supply chains and AI hardware imports — tariff cost on AI infrastructure capex is now a confirmed headwind. Data centre electricity cost inflation from energy shock erodes AI capex ROI. Private credit gate events undermine the cheap capital assumption behind AI capex funding models. AI capex remains both a debt amplifier (2026-2027 risk) and potential deflationary force (2028-2030).

flags.title: AI labour displacement beginning to show in entry-level employment data
flags.body: MAINTAINED: NBER Working Paper 34836: 502,000 AI-driven job cuts projected in 2026 — 9x the 55,000 in 2025. Critical dynamic: if realised, would push jobless_claims and consumer_confidence both RED, accelerating Consumer Staples BEARISH score. Fed cannot model AI displacement masked beneath aggregate labour statistics.

flags.title: EU CSRD Phase 2 creates mandatory climate risk disclosure for financial institutions
flags.body: MAINTAINED: CSRD Phase 2 in force since 1 January 2026; first reporting obligations 2027. Double materiality requirements expose EU financial institutions. Structural cost amplifier for G-SIB institutions already under capital pressure. Liberation Day tariffs add a parallel structural cost via supply chain compliance requirements.

flags.title: Iran/Hormuz conflict drives largest oil supply disruption in history — directly drives oil_supply_shock RED escalation
flags.body: MAINTAINED: Hormuz flows at ~4M bpd (vs normal 16M bpd). No AIS-confirmed commercial resumption. 5yr breakeven inflation +26bps since conflict onset — the energy shock is now directly embedded in CB policy. Sudan economic warfare and EM sovereign distress compounding. This is the dominant near-term driver of the GMM Energy score (-0.865 BEARISH) and key input into Consumer Staples and EM Equities.

flags.title: No material macro-financial cross-signal identified this period
flags.body: No FIMI-driven market manipulation identified. Hungary election resolved April 12 — outcome now known (watch for HUF and Hungarian sovereign spread reaction to post-election government formation).

flags.title: Hungary election April 12 — HUF and sovereign spread stress potential; tariff coercion as WDM-GMM structural link
flags.body: UPDATED: Hungary election April 12 is 5 days away. Tisza-Fidesz race remains tight. Macro relevance: Tisza victory + Russian economic retaliation scenario could spike HUF and Hungarian sovereign spreads. Additional WDM-GMM structural link: Liberation Day tariff package is politically driven economic coercion — WDM cms-005 flag (economic coercion as democratic erosion mechanism) is now confirmed at institutional level.

flags.title: US tariff shock (EU 20%) directly targets European economies — upgraded from NoSignal to Active
flags.body: UPGRADED: Liberation Day tariffs at 20% on EU are a direct economic coercion event — ESA cross-monitor relevance confirmed. ECB citing material near-term inflation from energy shock; EU Anti-Coercion Instrument filings initiated. Transatlantic decoupling protocol activated. Financial contagion from Treasury basis trade dislocation would affect ECB collateral availability. US-EU trade relationship is in structural transition — ESA designation upgraded from NoSignal to Active.

weekly_brief:
## Lead Signal

The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at **3.50-3.75%** during its April 28-29 FOMC meeting, citing elevated inflation partly reflecting recent increases in global energy prices and high uncertainty from Middle East conflict while noting weak job gains[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0001][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0002][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0003]. This dual mandate language signals policy paralysis rather than directional conviction, as the central bank remains frozen between inflation and employment objectives under a structurally elevated tariff regime and active energy price shock. Simultaneously, the US Trade Representative launched **76 new Section 301 investigations** covering forced labor enforcement and structural excess manufacturing capacity across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, the EU, and dozens of other economies, reconstituting tariff architecture on a more legally durable statutory basis following the Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against IEEPA-based tariffs[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0004]. This combination deepens stagflationary constraints on monetary policy and confirms tariff shock escalation rather than de-escalation, with the macro health composite score at **0.32** and system average conviction at **-0.686** indicating a stable CRISIS regime across all eight asset classes[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0018][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0019].

The European Central Bank reinforced this paralysis by holding all three key rates unchanged at its April 30 meeting, including the deposit facility rate at **2.00%**, driven by elevated near-term inflation from energy prices tied to Middle East conflict and a deteriorating growth outlook[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0005]. March 2026 staff projections revised headline inflation up to **2.6%** for 2026 while cutting growth to **0.9%**, constituting a stagflationary regime signal at the ECB level[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0006][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0007]. These developments anchor the inflation_central_bank and trade_tariff domains at Red stress levels with worsening trajectories, removing H2 2026 rate cut probabilities priced by markets.

## Other Developments

**US-China trade decoupling deepens with 28% import drop in 2025.** Peterson Institute analysis confirms real US imports from China dropped **28%** in 2025 alone, ending **40%** below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels, as China diversified agricultural sourcing with **80%** of soybean imports from Brazil and Argentina, up from **60%** in 2017[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0008][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0009]. US goods shipments to China fell to 2008-09 crisis levels after retaliation against Trump 2025 tariffs, prompting up to **11 billion dollars** in farmer subsidies from February 28, 2026, marking structural decoupling rather than cyclical adjustment[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0010]. Effective US tariff rates now average just over **12%**, up from **2.5%** pre-trade-war, with tariff escalation rung at **3** (enacted tariffs with active retaliation)[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0013][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0020].

**ECB Vice-President flags euro area liquidity stress.** ECB Vice-President de Guindos highlighted deteriorating bank access to money market funding and euro area central bank reserves nearly halved from 2022 peak to **2.6 trillion euros**[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0011][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0012]. This liquidity stress signal at the banking system level, combined with the stagflationary bind, elevates financial_stability and growth_recession domains to Amber with worsening direction, raising H2 2026 financial stability event probability if energy prices persist or sovereign debt stress emerges.

**Energy shock drives commodity price transmission at HIGH risk.** Both Fed and ECB cited energy inflation as primary policy constraint, with Brent crude at approximately **104 dollars per barrel** and Hormuz traffic at **5-10 ships per day** versus **138** normal, sustaining fragile ceasefire threats[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0014][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0015]. This anchors commodity price transmission vector at HIGH (red), with Reasoner confirming CRISIS regime stability.

**Jurisdictional stress deteriorates across majors.** United States, Euro Area, and China register ELEVATED overall stress with deteriorating trajectories, driven by Fed/ECB paralysis, USTR investigations, and import decoupling; Emerging Markets Aggregate hits HIGH external vulnerability amid USD debt loops and tariff/energy hits.

## Cross-Monitor Connections

This week macro signals link to environmental-risks via Hormuz fragility and Brent at **104 dollars per barrel** approaching contraction thresholds, amplifying commodity price transmission. Conflict-escalation connects through Fed and ECB citing Middle East uncertainty as policy constraint, tying energy shock to economic coercion. European-strategic-autonomy intersects with ECB liquidity stress (reserves at **2.6 trillion euros**) raising H2 financial contagion risks. Fimi-cognitive-warfare aligns with USTR **76 Section 301 investigations** signaling upward tariff trajectory despite market de-escalation misreads.

## Outlook

Monitor Section 301 investigation conclusions and new tariffs in Q3-Q4 2026, ECB June 10-11 forward guidance, and Hormuz/Brent movements over next 2-4 weeks to confirm trajectories. Standing alerts on dollar weaponization (DXY **98.50**, CFTC shorts 18th percentile), Treasury liquidity (**9.8 trillion dollars** maturity wall), and euro bank stress persist, with tail risks in Hormuz re-closure, Treasury refunding crisis, and EM defaults underweighted[gmm-int-2026-05-09-0016][gmm-int-2026-05-09-0017]. Material changes would elevate from CRISIS regime constraints.

module_0.title: Fed Frozen, Tariffs Reconstituted: Dual Mandate Paralysis Meets Section 301 Escalation
module_0.body: The Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50-3.75% at its April 28-29 FOMC meeting, citing elevated inflation from energy prices and Middle East uncertainty while noting weak job gains. The dual mandate language signals policy paralysis, not directional conviction. Simultaneously, USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations to rebuild tariff architecture on a more legally durable basis post-Supreme Court ruling. This combination deepens the stagflationary constraint on monetary policy and confirms the tariff shock is being escalated, not de-escalated. Watch for Section 301 investigation conclusions in Q3-Q4 2026 and any Fed forward guidance shift at June meeting.

mainstream.headline: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50-3.75% at April 28-29 FOMC meeting
mainstream.why_it_matters: The FOMC hold is not a neutral signal in the current regime context. With inflation elevated by energy prices and tariff pass-through still rippling through goods categories, the Fed is effectively frozen between its dual mandate legs. The statement language flagging attentiveness to risks on both sides of the dual mandate is a formulation that signals policy paralysis rather than directional conviction. This deepens the stagflationary holding pattern and removes the probability of rate cuts in H2 2026 that markets may be pricing.
mainstream.body: The FOMC hold is not a neutral signal in the current regime context. With inflation elevated by energy prices and tariff pass-through still rippling through goods categories, the Fed is effectively frozen between its dual mandate legs. The statement language flagging attentiveness to risks on both sides of the dual mandate is a formulation that signals policy paralysis rather than directional conviction. This deepens the stagflationary holding pattern and removes the probability of rate cuts in H2 2026 that markets may be pricing.
mainstream.asymmetric: Market may be pricing 1-2 cuts in H2 2026 based on growth slowdown fears; GMM assessment is that the Fed cannot cut while energy-driven inflation remains elevated and tariff pass-through to goods prices is still active. Divergence: market is front-running cuts that the dual mandate constraint does not yet permit.

mainstream.headline: ECB holds rates unchanged at April 30 meeting; energy inflation and Middle East conflict dominate outlook
mainstream.why_it_matters: The ECB Governing Council held all three key rates unchanged at its April 30 meeting, citing elevated near-term inflation driven by energy prices from the Middle East conflict and a deteriorating growth outlook. March 2026 staff projections revised headline inflation up to 2.6% for 2026 and cut growth to 0.9% for 2026. This simultaneous upward inflation revision and downward growth revision constitutes a stagflationary regime signal at the ECB level. ECB Vice-President de Guindos flagged deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves, nearly halved from 2022 peak to 2.6 trillion euros.
mainstream.body: The ECB Governing Council held all three key rates unchanged at its April 30 meeting, citing elevated near-term inflation driven by energy prices from the Middle East conflict and a deteriorating growth outlook. March 2026 staff projections revised headline inflation up to 2.6% for 2026 and cut growth to 0.9% for 2026. This simultaneous upward inflation revision and downward growth revision constitutes a stagflationary regime signal at the ECB level. ECB Vice-President de Guindos flagged deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves, nearly halved from 2022 peak to 2.6 trillion euros.
mainstream.asymmetric: ECB forward curve as of early 2026 was pricing no rate changes in either direction for 2026. GMM assessment: with inflation above 2% on energy and growth at 0.9%, the ECB is in a harder bind than markets are pricing. The probability of a rate hike to address energy-driven inflation is being underpriced relative to the stated risk of second-round effects.

mainstream.headline: US-China trade decoupling deepens: US imports from China down 28% in 2025
mainstream.why_it_matters: PIIE analysis of 2025 US import data confirms real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone, ending 40% below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels. China has actively diversified agricultural sourcing away from the US, with 80% of China soybean imports in 2025 coming from Brazil and Argentina, up from 60% in 2017. US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since the 2008-09 global financial crisis after China retaliated against Trump 2025 tariffs. The Trump administration responded with up to 11 billion dollars in farmer subsidies beginning February 28, 2026. This is a structural decoupling, not a cyclical trade adjustment.
mainstream.body: PIIE analysis of 2025 US import data confirms real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone, ending 40% below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels. China has actively diversified agricultural sourcing away from the US, with 80% of China soybean imports in 2025 coming from Brazil and Argentina, up from 60% in 2017. US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since the 2008-09 global financial crisis after China retaliated against Trump 2025 tariffs. The Trump administration responded with up to 11 billion dollars in farmer subsidies beginning February 28, 2026. This is a structural decoupling, not a cyclical trade adjustment.
mainstream.asymmetric: Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward. Divergence is material for sectors exposed to broad import competition.

underweighted.headline: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations to reconstitute tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling
underweighted.why_it_matters: Following the US Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against many of Trump 2025 IEEPA-based tariffs, the USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement (60 investigations) and structural excess manufacturing capacity (16 investigations) across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, the EU, and dozens of other economies. PIIE analysis assesses this as a deliberate attempt to rebuild tariff coverage on a more legally durable statutory basis, with new tariffs a likely outcome. Section 301 is characterized as harder to challenge legally than IEEPA because it is explicitly a tariff statute. This is a systematic reconstitution of the tariff architecture on a new statutory basis, not a de-escalation.
underweighted.body: Following the US Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against many of Trump 2025 IEEPA-based tariffs, the USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement (60 investigations) and structural excess manufacturing capacity (16 investigations) across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, the EU, and dozens of other economies. PIIE analysis assesses this as a deliberate attempt to rebuild tariff coverage on a more legally durable statutory basis, with new tariffs a likely outcome. Section 301 is characterized as harder to challenge legally than IEEPA because it is explicitly a tariff statute. This is a systematic reconstitution of the tariff architecture on a new statutory basis, not a de-escalation.
underweighted.asymmetric: Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward. Divergence is material for sectors exposed to broad import competition.

underweighted.headline: ECB Vice-President flags deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves
underweighted.why_it_matters: ECB Vice-President de Guindos, in his final parliamentary appearance, flagged deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves, nearly halved from 2022 peak to 2.6 trillion euros. This is a liquidity stress signal at the euro area banking system level, not a cyclical data wobble. Combined with the ECB stagflationary bind (inflation up, growth down), this raises the probability of a euro area financial stability event in H2 2026 if energy prices remain elevated or if a sovereign debt stress episode materializes.

vectors.name: Policy Rate Divergence
vectors.headline: Fed and ECB both frozen under dual mandate paralysis; no divergence but no easing path either
vectors.summary: Both Fed and ECB held rates unchanged this week, citing elevated inflation from energy prices and deteriorating growth outlook. This is not policy divergence but simultaneous policy paralysis at both central banks under stagflationary constraints.
vectors.detail: Federal Reserve held rates at 3.50-3.75% citing elevated inflation from energy prices and high uncertainty from Middle East conflict while noting weak job gains. ECB held rates unchanged citing elevated near-term inflation driven by energy prices and deteriorating growth outlook, with March 2026 staff projections revising headline inflation up to 2.6% for 2026 and cutting growth to 0.9% for 2026. Both central banks are frozen between inflation and growth mandates under structurally elevated tariff regime and active energy price shock. This is dual mandate paralysis at both central banks simultaneously, not a cyclical pause.
vectors.asymmetric: Market may be pricing 1-2 cuts in H2 2026 based on growth slowdown fears. GMM assessment is that neither Fed nor ECB can cut while energy-driven inflation remains elevated and tariff pass-through to goods prices is still active. Divergence: market is front-running cuts that the dual mandate constraint does not yet permit.

vectors.name: Trade Policy Shock
vectors.headline: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations to reconstitute tariff architecture; US-China decoupling deepens
vectors.summary: USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement and structural excess manufacturing capacity across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, EU, and dozens of other economies. PIIE analysis confirms real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone.
vectors.detail: Following the US Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against many of Trump 2025 IEEPA-based tariffs, the USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement (60 investigations) and structural excess manufacturing capacity (16 investigations) across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, the EU, and dozens of other economies. PIIE analysis assesses this as a deliberate attempt to rebuild tariff coverage on a more legally durable statutory basis, with new tariffs a likely outcome. PIIE analysis confirms real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone, ending 40% below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels. This is a structural decoupling, not a cyclical trade adjustment.
vectors.asymmetric: Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward. Divergence is material for sectors exposed to broad import competition.

items.asymmetric: Combined with the ECB stagflationary bind (inflation up, growth down), this raises the probability of a euro area financial stability event in H2 2026 if energy prices remain elevated or if a sovereign debt stress episode materializes. Euro area bank liquidity stress is not a mainstream narrative focus as of May 2026. module_9.title: Trade and Tariff Policy
module_9.digest_note: Tariff escalation rung upgraded to 3 with USTR Section 301 reconstitution; US-China decoupling deepening

new_developments.title: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations to reconstitute tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling
new_developments.actors: US — China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, EU, and dozens of other economies
new_developments.summary: Following the US Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against many of Trump 2025 IEEPA-based tariffs, the USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement (60 investigations) and structural excess manufacturing capacity (16 investigations) across China, Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, the EU, and dozens of other economies. PIIE analysis assesses this as a deliberate attempt to rebuild tariff coverage on a more legally durable statutory basis, with new tariffs a likely outcome. Section 301 is characterized as harder to challenge legally than IEEPA because it is explicitly a tariff statute.
new_developments.enforcement: Investigations launched; new tariffs likely outcome in Q3-Q4 2026 as investigations conclude
new_developments.asymmetric: Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward. Divergence is material for sectors exposed to broad import competition.

new_developments.title: US-China trade decoupling deepens: US imports from China down 28% in 2025
new_developments.summary: PIIE analysis of 2025 US import data confirms real US imports from China dropped 28% in 2025 alone, ending 40% below pre-first-trade-war 2018 levels. China has actively diversified agricultural sourcing away from the US, with 80% of China soybean imports in 2025 coming from Brazil and Argentina, up from 60% in 2017. US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since the 2008-09 global financial crisis after China retaliated against Trump 2025 tariffs. The Trump administration responded with up to 11 billion dollars in farmer subsidies beginning February 28, 2026.
new_developments.enforcement: Structural decoupling in force; farmer subsidies active
new_developments.asymmetric: This is a structural decoupling, not a cyclical trade adjustment. Markets may be treating the Supreme Court ruling as a de-escalation signal. GMM assessment: the ruling accelerated tariff reconstitution on a more durable legal basis. The net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward.

module_9.friction_analysis: Trade friction is highest in US-China bilateral relationship, with 28% import drop in 2025 and active agricultural diversification by China away from US sourcing. USTR Section 301 investigations extend friction to Vietnam, Taiwan, Mexico, Japan, and EU, signaling broad-based tariff reconstitution rather than bilateral de-escalation. Effective US tariff rate now averages just over 12%, up from around 2.5% pre-trade-war.
module_9.tariff_escalation_rung: Tariff escalation rung upgraded to 3 (Enacted tariffs with active retaliation). USTR Section 301 investigations signal intent to rebuild tariff coverage on more legally durable statutory basis following Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against IEEPA-based tariffs. New tariffs likely outcome in Q3-Q4 2026 as investigations conclude. Critical path: watch for Section 301 investigation conclusions and any new tariff announcements in Q3-Q4 2026.

retaliation_chain.measure: Agricultural diversification away from US sourcing; 80% of China soybean imports in 2025 from Brazil and Argentina
retaliation_chain.macro_impact: US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since 2008-09 global financial crisis; Trump administration responded with up to 11 billion dollars in farmer subsidies beginning February 28, 2026

central_bank_coordination.summary: Both Fed and ECB held rates unchanged this week, citing elevated inflation from energy prices and deteriorating growth outlook. This is not policy divergence but simultaneous policy paralysis at both central banks under stagflationary constraints. Market may be pricing 1-2 cuts in H2 2026 based on growth slowdown fears. GMM assessment is that neither Fed nor ECB can cut while energy-driven inflation remains elevated and tariff pass-through to goods prices is still active.

governance_gaps.gap: No multilateral coordination mechanism for energy price shock transmission under tariff regime
governance_gaps.analysis: Both Fed and ECB are frozen under dual mandate paralysis driven by energy price shock and tariff pass-through. No multilateral coordination mechanism exists to address energy price shock transmission under structurally elevated tariff regime. This governance gap raises the probability of uncoordinated policy responses and financial stability events in H2 2026.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: If Hormuz re-closes or Brent exceeds 115 dollars per barrel sustained, the absence of a multilateral coordination mechanism for energy price shock transmission could trigger uncoordinated policy responses and financial stability events. This governance gap is underweighted relative to its structural significance.

Combined with ECB stagflationary bind, this is a classic precursor to a financial stability event but not a mainstream narrative focus as of May 2026.

active_tactical_alerts.name: Dollar Weaponization
active_tactical_alerts.description: DXY 98.50 with CFTC shorts at 18th percentile signals short-squeeze risk, but SA fiscal fragility (9.8 trillion dollar maturity wall, 1 trillion dollar plus interest costs) supports current WARNING assessment. No new weekly developments alter this standing regime assessment.

active_tactical_alerts.name: Treasury Liquidity
active_tactical_alerts.description: 9.8 trillion dollar maturity wall with 800 billion dollars per month refinancing amid 1 trillion dollar interest costs meets limited bank reserve cushion. NY Fed notes war-re-elevated volatility. 30yr April 9 shortfall precedent suggests building stress.

active_tactical_alerts.name: FX Swap Basis
active_tactical_alerts.description: Q1 volumes ATH 3.6 trillion dollars; partial ceasefire normalization shows ELEVATED but lacks SA corroboration for improvement. TS spike episodic noise not warranting asset regime shift. Downgraded from WARNING to WATCH.

open_findings.headline: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50-3.75% citing elevated inflation from energy prices and high uncertainty from Middle East conflict
open_findings.why_it_matters: Dual mandate language signals policy paralysis rather than directional conviction; Fed is frozen between inflation and employment mandates under structurally elevated tariff regime and active energy price shock

open_findings.headline: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations to reconstitute tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling
open_findings.why_it_matters: Systematic reconstitution of tariff architecture on more legally durable statutory basis following Supreme Court February 2026 ruling against IEEPA-based tariffs; net tariff trajectory is upward, not downward

open_findings.headline: ECB holds rates unchanged citing elevated near-term inflation driven by energy prices and deteriorating growth outlook
open_findings.why_it_matters: Simultaneous upward inflation revision to 2.6% for 2026 and downward growth revision to 0.9% for 2026 constitutes stagflationary regime signal at ECB level

open_findings.headline: ECB Vice-President flags deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves
open_findings.why_it_matters: Liquidity stress signal at euro area banking system level; combined with ECB stagflationary bind, raises probability of euro area financial stability event in H2 2026 if energy prices remain elevated

open_findings.headline: US-China trade decoupling deepens: US imports from China down 28% in 2025
open_findings.why_it_matters: Structural decoupling, not cyclical trade adjustment; China has actively diversified agricultural sourcing away from US; US goods shipments to China fell to levels not seen since 2008-09 global financial crisis

proposed_patches.material_change_justification: ECB Vice-President de Guindos flagged deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves, nearly halved from 2022 peak to 2.6 trillion euros. This is a new liquidity stress signal at the euro area banking system level warranting upgrade of Liquidity Fragmentation and Banking Sector Stress vectors from ELEVATED to ELEVATED with changed flag.

USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations to reconstitute tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling. This is a systematic reconstitution of the tariff architecture on a new statutory basis warranting upgrade of Trade Policy Shock vector from HIGH to HIGH with changed flag.

System average stable at -0.686; all 8 asset classes BEARISH/MILD NEGATIVE with no deviations from baselines; FOMC and ECB dual mandate paralysis plus USTR Section 301 tariff reconstitution deepen CRISIS regime constraints. Append new conviction_history entry for 2026-05-09.

ECB Vice-President flags deteriorating bank access to money market funding and declining euro area central bank reserves. This is a new structural gap in mainstream narrative focus warranting addition of BS-003 Euro Area Bank Liquidity Stress Underweighted to blind_spots.

FX swap basis ELEVATED shows partial normalization on ceasefire but lacks SA corroboration for improvement. TS spike episodic noise not warranting asset regime shift. Downgrade TA-003 FX Swap Basis from WARNING to WATCH.
